nameMeanings.name: Joane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Joane was born about 1572, in Kent, England. She married Richard Legatt on 19 September 1597, in Boxgrove, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. She died after 21 August 1639, in Epping, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Saint James,Bury Saint Edmunds,Suffolk,E: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈 스트 자메스
ko.lifeSummary: Joane(은)는 대략 1572년 영국 켄트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1597년 9월 19일 영국 수세스 보스그로브에서 Richard Legatt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1639년 8월 21일 이후 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 핀그에서 사망했다.

ru.Saint James,Bury Saint Edmunds,Suffolk,E: бури ст эдмундс ст джемесь, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Joane родилась около 1572 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Legatt 19 сентября 1597 г. в местности боксгров, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла после 21 августа 1639 г. в местности эппинг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Theydon Garnon, Essex, ENGLAND: Theydon Garnon, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Joane nació aproximadamente 1572, en Kent, Inglaterra. Se casó con Richard Legatt el 19 de septiembre de 1597, en Boxgrove, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió después de 21 de agosto de 1639, en Epping, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
